title: Getting Started with hyperliquid
Getting Started with hyperliquid
text: Public /info reads (market data, account state, staking reads) need credentials. Signed /exchange actions (trading, transfers, staking writes) authenticated Ethereum wallet signature: Hyperliquid uses API keys, walletãs secp256k1 private key. Store key environment variable .Renviron: get_api_keys() reads variables (derives wallet address key), practice simply call HyperliquidTrading$new() credentials picked automatically. One key, one flag network. key serves mainnet testnet. network selected explicit testnet argument construction, never URL, network changes signature (phantom-agent source hyperliquidChain tag part signed). Pass testnet = TRUE sign route testnet.
code: HYPERLIQUID_PRIVATE_KEY="0x<64-hex-character-secp256k1-key>" # Optional: the master account address when the key above is an AGENT (API) # wallet approved to act on its behalf. HYPERLIQUID_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS="0x<master-account-address>" box::use(   hyperliquid[     HyperliquidMarketData, HyperliquidAccount, HyperliquidTrading, get_api_keys   ] )  keys <- get_api_keys(   private_key = "0x<64-hex-character-secp256k1-key>" )

title: Getting Started with hyperliquid
text: Every method returns single flat data.table list columns: Nested API objects flattened scalar snake_case columns (e.g.ô positionãs leverage becomes leverage_type leverage_value). Endpoints bundle two things split sibling methods payload (get_positions() / get_margin_summary()). Heterogeneous rows stacked discriminator column: L2 bookãs side, order actionãs status, ledgerãs delta_type. Prices, sizes, amounts parsed numeric; timestamps POSIXct UTC (via lubridate). means result call drops straight data.table joins, rbindlist(), database write unnesting.

title: Getting Started with hyperliquid
text: Every class works async mode. Pass async = TRUE method returns promise instead data.table. recommended idiom coro::async() / await() sequential-looking code, driving event loop later:
code: market_async <- HyperliquidMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  main <- coro$async(function() {   mids <- await(market_async$get_all_mids())   book <- await(market_async$get_l2_book("BTC"))    print(mids)   print(book)   return(invisible(NULL)) })  main()  # Drain the event loop until every promise has resolved. while (!later$loop_empty()) {   later$run_now() } #>      coin        mid #>    <char>      <num> #> 1:    BTC 61958.5000 #> 2:    ETH  1605.4500 #> 3:     @1     9.6738 #>      side level    px       sz     n #>    <char> <int> <num>    <num> <num> #> 1:    bid     1 61945  0.03164     3 #> 2:    bid     2 61944  0.00085     4 #> 3:    ask     1 61946 13.32523    39 #> 4:    ask     2 61947  0.06724     6

title: Funding a Wallet and Running a Live Testnet Trade
text: packageãs signing already proven correct two ways money involved: Byte--byte test vectors. tests/testthat/test-signing.R reproduces official Hyperliquid SDK signatures exactly. zero-funds live check. Posting signed order brand-new wallet makes exchange reply "User API Wallet 0xãÎ exist" ã address echoes back one recovered signature. matches wallet, entire build ã sign ã send ã verify path confirmed without spending anything. funded run proves one extra thing: order actually fills end end. optional. need confidence code correct, already .

what: Step 1 ã A wallet to trade from
title: Funding a Wallet and Running a Live Testnet Trade
text: used fresh MetaMask browser extension (Hyperliquidãs web app connects browser wallets cleanly). mobile wallet Exodus can connect via WalletConnect, desktop extension far smoother.

what: Step 2 ã Getting money onto the right ãlaneã (the confusing part)
title: Funding a Wallet and Running a Live Testnet Trade
text: Hyperliquidãs deposit screen asks ãUSDC Arbitrum,ã newcomers (including us) get stuck. vocabulary: USDC stablecoin ã digital US dollar, worth ~$1. money Hyperliquid account trades . Arbitrum company, platform, account ã nothing sign KYC. network: think cheaper express lane built top Ethereum. Hyperliquid accepts USDC sitting Arbitrum lane. HYPE Hyperliquidãs token (staking, fees, governance, trading). cash account trades , holding HYPE fund account. Snag hit #1 ã ãthought Ethereum just Ethereum.ã now one main Ethereum network plus several cheaper ãlayer-2ã lanes built (Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, ãÎ). coin can live different lanes, coin one lane used another bridged across. Snag #2 ã fees paid ETH, USDC. Every move network costs fee (ãgasã), lane charges gas ETH. USDC started main Ethereum lane, first needed little ETH Ethereum move ã later little ETH Arbitrum deposit. Moving $9 ended needing ~$1 ETH gas, swap ETH onto Arbitrum. Snag #3 ã main Ethereum lane expensive. Moving small amounts Ethereum mainnet can cost several dollars gas. Cheaper ways land USDC little ETH directly Arbitrum (skipping costly hops): centralised exchange withdrawal, choosing Arbitrum network (flat fee, slippage) ã needs KYC account. dedicated bridge Across Jumper (tighter pricing walletãs built-bridge). wallets support Arbitrum directly can send MetaMask Arbitrum. switch USDC Ethereum Arbitrum used MetaMaskãs built-Bridge (ãswapã): USDC Ethereum USDC Arbitrum. Snag #4 ã MetaMaskãs swap/bridge takes cut. charges service fee (~0.9%) plus slippage. Convenient, cheapest route (see bridges ).

what: Step 3 ã Deposit on Hyperliquid mainnet
title: Funding a Wallet and Running a Live Testnet Trade
text: USDC (little ETH gas) Arbitrum, Hyperliquid Deposit screen works: minimum deposit $5 USDC ã anything less credited. small (~$0.20) deposit fee. deposit Arbitrum transaction, needs tiny bit Arbitrum ETH gas (cents). deposited ~$9. mainnet deposit unlock testnet faucet ã actual test trades use free testnet money, reason deposit .

title: Funding a Wallet and Running a Live Testnet Trade

Connect wallet click ãClaim 1000 mock USDC.ã Snag #5 ã ãclaimed MetaMask shows nothing.ã Correct, expected. MetaMask shows coins wallet. mock USDC balance inside Hyperliquid account (like money shown exchangeãs website), testnet money exists testnet site. see , open testnet site ã Trade page look account panel (Portfolio page showed $0 us; Trade panel showed ãAvailable trade ~999 USDCã). can also confirm directly, bypassing UI entirely, public /info request ã key needed:

what: Step 5 ã Make a safe key to trade with (API / agent wallet)
title: Funding a Wallet and Running a Live Testnet Trade
text: let package sign behalf without ever handling real key, Hyperliquid provides API wallets (also called agent wallets): keys can place trades withdraw. testnet site, open ã API, name wallet, click Generate, authorise (main wallet signs approval MetaMask). Snag #6 ã address vs.ô private key. ãGenerateã shows two different strings. address 0x + 40 characters (public). private key 0x + 64 characters (secret, shown ãsave ã warning). package needs private key. easy copy wrong one. Put API key gitignored .Renviron (never commit ). agent wallet, also set main account address reads order attribution point funded account: .Renviron.example template ships package. can sanity-check key matches agent address authorised:
code: # .Renviron  (chmod 600, listed in .gitignore -- do not commit) # HYPERLIQUID_PRIVATE_KEY=0x<the 64-char API/agent key> # HYPERLIQUID_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS=0x<your main account address> box::use(ethsign[eth_address]) eth_address("0x<the API/agent key>")  # should equal the authorised agent address

what: Step 6 ã Place a real order, watch it fill
title: Funding a Wallet and Running a Live Testnet Trade
text: Construct trading client credentials place order. couple testnet realities shaped : Snag #7 ã ãUnifiedã account, agents canãt move funds. faucet USDC landed Spot balance, perps trade Perps balance. Normally transfer Spot ã Perps, account Unified mode , merges two ã spot collateral backs perps directly transfer needed. (Worth knowing: agent wallet Spotãÿ¡Perps transfer anyway ã main wallet can; exchange returns ãMust deposit performing actionsã agent.) Snag #8 ã ãPrice far oracle.ã plain market order (market_open()) prices percent book. testnet oracle price runs ~2ã3% away live book, buffer pushed price outside Hyperliquidãs allowed band rejected. fix price precise limit order current book instead: Snag #9 ã IOC ãmatch.ã thin, fast-moving testnet book, Immediate--Cancel order priced stale ask can miss cancel. Reading book immediately ordering (/using GTC unfilled remainder rests) makes fill reliable. round-trip ã open, hold real position, close ã whole proof: package built, signed (agent key), submitted, exchange matched trade, live.
code: box::use(hyperliquid[HyperliquidTrading, get_api_keys])  readRenviron(".Renviron") trading <- HyperliquidTrading$new(keys = get_api_keys(), testnet = TRUE)  # A short sell is just is_buy = FALSE; a long is is_buy = TRUE. (Pairs trading is # one of each on two markets.) Here: a small marketable BUY priced at the ask. trading$place_order(   name = "BTC", is_buy = TRUE, sz = 0.0002, limit_px = 64219,   order_type = list(limit = list(tif = "Gtc")) ) #> status filled, avg_px 64119  -> long 0.0002 BTC  # Flatten it with a reduce-only sell. trading$place_order(   name = "BTC", is_buy = FALSE, sz = 0.0002, limit_px = 63964,   order_type = list(limit = list(tif = "Gtc")), reduce_only = TRUE ) #> status filled  -> position closed, account flat

title: Trading Strategies: Long, Short, and Pairs Trading
text: Spot trading means buying owning asset (hold coin). perpetual future (ãperpã) contract tracks assetãs price without ever expiring. never coin; hold position gains loses price moves. Perps core Hyperliquid. Long = profit price goes (is_buy = TRUE). Short = profit price goes (is_buy = FALSE). short first-class position, workaround. Leverage lets small amount collateral control larger position (e.g. 5x). magnifies gains losses, can trigger liquidation (position force-closed) adverse move. Funding small payment exchanged longs shorts every hours keep perp price tethered spot price.

what: Opening a position, and the order types
title: Trading Strategies: Long, Short, and Pairs Trading
text: HyperliquidTrading signs sends orders. two everyday order types: Limit order ã rests book price choose; fills price better. time--force controls lives: "Gtc" (good-til-cancelled, default), "Ioc" (immediate--cancel ã fill now die), "Alo" (add-liquidity-/ post-ã never crosses spread). Market order ã fills immediately crossing spread (market_open() implements aggressive IOC).
code: trading <- HyperliquidTrading$new(keys = KEYS)  # A good-til-cancelled limit BUY (a long): rest a bid at 60,000. trading$place_order(   name = "BTC", is_buy = TRUE, sz = 0.001, limit_px = 60000,   order_type = list(limit = list(tif = "Gtc")) )  # Or take liquidity immediately with a market buy. trading$market_open(name = "BTC", is_buy = TRUE, sz = 0.001) #>     status      oid total_sz avg_px                                 error #>     <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>                                <char> #> 1: resting 77738308       NA     NA                                  <NA> #> 2:  filled 77747314     0.02 1891.4                                  <NA> #> 3:   error       NA       NA     NA Order must have minimum value of $10. #>     status      oid total_sz avg_px                                 error #>     <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>                                <char> #> 1: resting 77738308       NA     NA                                  <NA> #> 2:  filled 77747314     0.02 1891.4                                  <NA> #> 3:   error       NA       NA     NA Order must have minimum value of $10.

title: Trading Strategies: Long, Short, and Pairs Trading
text: strategy motivated example. pairs trade holds one leg long one leg short time ã profit relative move two, marketãs overall direction. long BTC short ETH BTC outperforms ETH, win even fall, short leg gains long leg loses less (vice-versa). Open legs ã long short ã whatever order types prefer (: resting limit long, market fill short): filled, account holds two opposing legs. get_positions() returns one row per open position, signed szi (positive = long, negative = short): real testnet run exact pair filled long 0.0002 BTC @ $64,424 short 0.01 ETH @ $1,688.9 ã balanced spread. spreadãs profit loss difference two legsã moves.
code: # Leg 1: LONG BTC (limit). trading$place_order(   name = "BTC", is_buy = TRUE, sz = 0.002, limit_px = 62000,   order_type = list(limit = list(tif = "Gtc")) )  # Leg 2: SHORT ETH (market). trading$market_open(name = "ETH", is_buy = FALSE, sz = 0.4) #>     status      oid total_sz avg_px                                 error #>     <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>                                <char> #> 1: resting 77738308       NA     NA                                  <NA> #> 2:  filled 77747314     0.02 1891.4                                  <NA> #> 3:   error       NA       NA     NA Order must have minimum value of $10. #>     status      oid total_sz avg_px                                 error #>     <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>                                <char> #> 1: resting 77738308       NA     NA                                  <NA> #> 2:  filled 77747314     0.02 1891.4                                  <NA> #> 3:   error       NA       NA     NA Order must have minimum value of $10. account <- HyperliquidAccount$new(keys = KEYS) account$get_positions()[, .(coin, szi, entry_px, position_value, unrealized_pnl)] #>      coin      szi entry_px position_value unrealized_pnl #>    <char>    <num>    <num>          <num>          <num> #> 1:    BTC  0.61148 61699.10     37899.5304       171.7548 #> 2:    ETH -0.37080  1606.85       595.6902         0.1306

what: Protecting a position: take-profit and stop-loss
title: Trading Strategies: Long, Short, and Pairs Trading
text: trigger order fires market reaches triggerPx. Use tpsl = "tp" take profit, tpsl = "sl" stop loss; isMarket = TRUE fills market triggered. Make reduce_only can close position.
code: # Stop-loss on the BTC long: if BTC trades down to 60,000, market-sell to exit. trading$place_order(   name = "BTC", is_buy = FALSE, sz = 0.002, limit_px = 60000,   order_type = list(trigger = list(isMarket = TRUE, triggerPx = 60000, tpsl = "sl")),   reduce_only = TRUE ) #>     status      oid total_sz avg_px                                 error #>     <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>                                <char> #> 1: resting 77738308       NA     NA                                  <NA> #> 2:  filled 77747314     0.02 1891.4                                  <NA> #> 3:   error       NA       NA     NA Order must have minimum value of $10.

what: API Wrapper to the Hyperliquid Exchange
title: API Wrapper to the Hyperliquid Exchange
text: R API wrapper Hyperliquid decentralised exchange supporting synchronous asynchronous (promise based) operations. Provides R6 classes market data, perpetual spot trading, account management, transfers, staking. Every method returns flat data.table. /exchange endpoint authenticated Ethereum wallet signature, computed pure R via companion ethsign package (compiled code) verified byte--byte official Hyperliquid SDKs.

title: API Wrapper to the Hyperliquid Exchange
text: software provided educational research purposes. Trading cryptocurrency carries substantial risk, solely responsible orders, transfers, withdrawals placed package. Test testnet (testnet = TRUE) signing anything mainnet.

title: API Wrapper to the Hyperliquid Exchange
text: One method, one data.table, list columns. Every method returns flat data.table; nested API objects flattened scalar columns, heterogeneous rows stacked discriminator column (side, delta_type, status, ãÎ). Sync async. Every method works modes. async = TRUE returns promise; otherwise results returned directly. single sync/async branch point (hyperliquid_build_request()). snake_case columns. API camelCase fields become snake_case columns; prices sizes returned numerics. Pure-R Ethereum signing via github.com/dereckscompany/ethsign. secp256k1 ECDSA, Keccak-256, EIP-712 come standalone pure-R ethsign package; Hyperliquid-specific msgpack action hash lives . compiled code, verified byte-identical test vectors official Hyperliquid Python SDK (v0.24.0), used reference implementation. One host, two paths. whole REST API lives behind one host: /info (public reads) /exchange (signed writes). network chosen explicit testnet flag, never URL sniffing, network changes signature .

title: API Wrapper to the Hyperliquid Exchange
text: Public market-data account reads (/info endpoint) need credentials. Signed /exchange actions (trading, transfers, staking writes) authenticated Ethereum wallet signature: Hyperliquid uses API keys, walletãs secp256k1 private key. call signed method, package builds action, hashes , signs locally key, sends resulting signature request body ã private key never leaves machine never transmitted Hyperliquid. low-level signing primitives (secp256k1 ECDSA, Keccak-256, EIP-712) factored small standalone package, ethsign, package builds ; read want mechanics reuse Ethereum signing elsewhere. (cryptography library ã just uses one.) Store key environment variable .Renviron: get_api_keys() reads variables (derives wallet address key): key serves networks. network selected explicit testnet flag construction, URL, network changes signature (phantom-agent source hyperliquidChain tag part signed):
code: HYPERLIQUID_PRIVATE_KEY="0x<64-hex-character-secp256k1-key>" # Optional: the master account address when the key above is an AGENT (API) # wallet approved to act on its behalf. HYPERLIQUID_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS="0x<master-account-address>" box::use(hyperliquid[get_api_keys])  keys <- get_api_keys() # Sign and route against testnet trading <- HyperliquidTrading$new(testnet = TRUE)

text: package written around promises non-blocking, event-loop use. Pass async = TRUE class methods return promise instead data.table. Resolve coro::async() / await() sequential-looking code, drive event loop later.
code: market_async <- HyperliquidMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  main <- coro$async(function() {   mids <- await(market_async$get_all_mids())   book <- await(market_async$get_l2_book("BTC"))    print(mids)   print(book)   return(invisible(NULL)) })  main()  # Drain the event loop until every promise has resolved. while (!later$loop_empty()) {   later$run_now() } #>      coin        mid #>    <char>      <num> #> 1:    BTC 61958.5000 #> 2:    ETH  1605.4500 #> 3:     @1     9.6738 #>      side level    px       sz     n #>    <char> <int> <num>    <num> <num> #> 1:    bid     1 61945  0.03164     3 #> 2:    bid     2 61944  0.00085     4 #> 3:    ask     1 61946 13.32523    39 #> 4:    ask     2 61947  0.06724     6

title: HyperliquidBase: Abstract Base Class for Hyperliquid API Clients ã HyperliquidBase
text: Provides shared infrastructure Hyperliquid R6 classes: wallet credentials, network selection, sync/async execution mode, request funnel (hyperliquid_build_request()), lazy exchange-metadata caching.

title: HyperliquidBase: Abstract Base Class for Hyperliquid API Clients ã HyperliquidBase
text: async parameter controls execution mode API methods: async = FALSE (default): methods return results directly. async = TRUE: methods return promises::promise objects resolve types. Async mode requires promises package (Suggests). Consume promises coro::async() await() promises::(); drive event loop script use (optional) later package, e.g. (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now().

title: HyperliquidBase: Abstract Base Class for Hyperliquid API Clients ã HyperliquidBase
text: entire REST API lives behind one host two POST paths: /info (public reads, unauthenticated) /exchange (writes, authenticated wallet signature body). network differs mainnet testnet, chosen explicit testnet flag, never URL sniffing: network changes signature (phantom-agent source hyperliquidChain tag). See get_base_url().

title: HyperliquidBase: Abstract Base Class for Hyperliquid API Clients ã HyperliquidBase

hyperliquid 0.0.1
text: Initial release: R wrapper Hyperliquid decentralised exchange, covering perpetual spot trading across synchronous asynchronous (promise-based) operations, Ethereum wallet signing /exchange endpoint computed pure R.

title: hyperliquid 0.0.1
text: Market data (HyperliquidMarketData, public, auth): perp spot metadata, per-asset contexts (mark/mid/oracle/funding/open-interest), mids, L2 order book, OHLCV candles, funding history predicted fundings, builder-deployed perp dexes, recent trades, exchange status. Account (HyperliquidAccount, public reads address): positions cross-margin summary, spot balances, open frontend orders, fills (recent time), historical orders, funding non-funding ledgers, portfolio value/PnL/volume, fee schedule, rate limit, role, sub-accounts, single-order status, vault equities. Trading (HyperliquidTrading, signed): place / modify / cancel orders (single bulk, order id client order id), market open close, scheduled dead-manãs-switch cancel, leverage isolated-margin updates, agent / builder-fee approvals. Transfers (HyperliquidTransfers, signed): spot/perp collateral class transfer, USDC spot-token sends, bridge withdrawals, cross-dex sends, sub-account vault transfers. Staking (HyperliquidStaking): delegator summary, per-validator delegations, reward full delegate/deposit/withdraw history, delegate / undelegate action. Bulk backfill: hyperliquid_backfill_klines() hyperliquid_backfill_funding() walk history many coins/intervals CSV, appending incrementally resume. bundled hyperliquid_ohlcv sample dataset ships daily candles examples. Sync async: every method works modes; async = TRUE returns promises::promise resolving data.table.

title: hyperliquid 0.0.1
text: Every public method returns single flat data.table list columns; nested objects flattened scalar snake_case columns heterogeneous rows stacked discriminator column. /exchange endpoint authenticated Ethereum wallet signature (secp256k1 ECDSA, Keccak-256, EIP-712, msgpack action hash) implemented pure R (openssl + gmp) compiled code, verified byte-identical official Python Rust SDK test vectors. requests flow single funnel (hyperliquid_build_request()) one sync/async branch point. network selected explicit testnet flag, never URL sniffing, changes signature . Inputs validated assert package; times handled UTC via lubridate, amounts transmitted exact precision.
